question: Does FINMA's practice on consolidated supervision of financial groups also apply, mutatis mutandis, to financial groups dominated by securities firms?
section: I. Subject and scope of application

question: A financial group operates primarily in the banking sector. What must it additionally include, at minimum, in order to qualify as a financial conglomerate?
gold: insurance company of substantial economic importance
accept: an insurance company of substantial economic importance
at least one insurance company of substantial economic importance
section: II. Consolidated supervision / A. Scope of application

question: If a financial group exists in the legal sense, is consolidated supervision generally assumed?
section: B. Interpretation of the provisions of Articles 3b and 3d BA

question: When licensing an institution that belongs to a foreign financial group, may FINMA require appropriate consolidated supervision by a foreign supervisory authority?
section: C. Ring-fencing measures are an exception

question: Which categories of ring-fencing and other measures are available to FINMA in individual cases?
gold: information and reporting obligations to FINMA
accept: restrictions on access to group systems
information and reporting obligations to FINMA
section: C. Ring-fencing measures are an exception

question: Is a company's business activity the only factor that determines whether it falls within the regulatory scope of consolidation?
accept: no, control or support obligations also matter

question: Must significant consolidation decisions made by an institution be reported to FINMA even when the scope of consolidation remains unchanged?
For the purposes of consolidated supervision, does activity in the insurance sector generally count as activity in the financial sector?
section: B. Activity in the financial sector

question: Does the technology a company uses play a role in assessing whether it provides or intermediates services for financial transactions?
accept: no, substance over form applies irrespective of technology
section: B. Activity in the financial sector

question: Through which forms of linkage can companies operating in the financial sector form an interconnected system?
gold: de facto obligation to provide support
accept: de facto obligation to provide support

question: Besides holding a majority of votes or capital, through which circumstances in particular can one company control another by other means?
gold: voting rights agreement with other shareholders
right to appoint or remove a majority of the supreme body
accept: voting rights agreement with other shareholders
control of voting rights by agreement
right to appoint or remove a majority of the supreme body
right to appoint or remove a majority of the supreme management or administrative body
appointment and removal rights over the top body
significant influence on executive management or business policy

question: A group company of a bank is neither majority-owned nor otherwise controlled, but legal and factual elements indicate a duty to provide support. Can the company still be included in consolidated supervision?
From which circumstances in particular can a legal or de facto obligation to provide support arise?
gold: strategic, personnel, organisational or financial interdependencies
letters of comfort or keepwell agreements
accept: strategic, personnel, organisational or financial interdependencies
letters of comfort or keepwell agreements

question: Where an institution holds a higher participation rate in a company, can weaker additional links suffice to justify including that company in consolidated supervision?
At least how many institutions must the holding company at the head of a financial group control?
Which conditions must a holding company satisfy in order to be regarded as immaterial for the consolidated supervision of a financial group?
gold: controls no other financial-sector company besides the institution
no own financial activities beyond holding the interest in the institution
no influence on the institution's business activities
accept: controls no other financial-sector company besides the institution
no control of other companies active in the financial sector
does not control any other financial-sector company
no own financial activities beyond holding the interest in the institution
no financial activities of its own
does not engage in own financial activities apart from the holding
no influence on the institution's business activities
does not influence the institution's business activities
not leveraged to any significant extent

question: When an immaterial holding company is left out of the regulatory scope of consolidation, who comments on whether the exclusion criteria are met on a permanent basis?

A bank is controlled by a holding company whose qualifying holdings are predominantly in companies outside the financial sector. Does FINMA examine whether measures to ring-fence the bank from the rest of the group are necessary?
Which group structures does FINMA regard in particular as atypical structures or interconnected systems?
As what kind of group structure are central organisations and their affiliated institutions typically constituted?

An institution organised as a partnership belongs to a de facto financial group. Must the audit report address whether its partners with unlimited liability control other companies active in the financial sector?
Does FINMA's consolidated supervision in principle extend to group companies of a financial group that are not active in the financial sector?
Who designates the significant group companies of a banking group and keeps a list of them?
section: a) Group companies with activities pursuant to Article 3a BO

question: A financial group sets up a special purpose vehicle in an unusual legal form. Can the SPV nevertheless fall within the regulatory scope of consolidation?
May the regulatory scope of consolidation differ from the scope of consolidation used for the consolidated financial statements?
section: F. Distinction between the scope of consolidation for the consolidated

question: On a consolidated basis, are financial groups generally subject to the same provisions as institutions on an individual basis?
section: IV. Content of consolidated supervision (group-wide provisions)

question: The content of consolidated supervision can be divided into which two categories of requirements?
section: IV. Content of consolidated supervision (group-wide provisions)

question: Does the required separation between the group-level management body and the body responsible for governance, supervision and control also apply to financial groups dominated by securities firms acting as market makers?

Two supervised institutions within the same financial group want to appoint regulatory audit firms from different networks for their single-entity audits. Is this permissible without FINMA authorisation?
accept: no, FINMA must authorise an exception

question: How are capital requirements determined for financial groups dominated by persons under Article 1b of the Banking Act?
accept: appropriate requirements imposed case by case

question: Are subordinated financial groups generally subject to the duty to consolidate for liquidity purposes?
Which body of law exclusively governs group accounting for financial groups dominated by persons under Article 1b of the Banking Act?
accept: Art. 963 ff. of the Code of Obligations

question: Apart from the institution itself, a financial group consists only of immaterial group companies. Is a fundamental exemption from the quantitative elements of consolidated supervision possible?
section: C. Criteria for exemption from the quantitative elements for group

question: Only some of a financial group's companies are immaterial. Are all group companies then released from the quantitative elements of consolidated supervision?
accept: no, only the immaterial entities are excluded
section: C. Criteria for exemption from the quantitative elements for group

question: Several group companies of a financial group are each immaterial when viewed individually. Can they nevertheless be material as a whole?
section: C. Criteria for exemption from the quantitative elements for group
